seed_instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals.
org_constraint: Begin the first paragraph with the phrase "I hope this email finds you well."
new_constraint: Begin the first paragraph with a question rather than a greeting.
instruction: Create an email to a professor introducing yourself and your goals. Begin the first paragraph with the phrase "I hope this email finds you well." Begin the first paragraph with a question rather than a greeting.
response:
Subject: Introduction - [Your Name] - [Course Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well. As a student enrolled in your [Course Name] class this semester, I wanted to take a moment to introduce myself and share a bit about my academic interests. I am [Your Name], and I am currently a [Your Year, e.g., sophomore, graduate student] majoring in [Your Major].

I have always been fascinated by [mention a specific area related to the course or professor's research]. What particularly drew me to your course, [Course Name], is [mention something specific about the course, e.g., the focus on X theory, the opportunity to study Y case studies]. My long-term goal is to [state your career or academic goal, e.g., pursue a Ph.D. in environmental science, work in data analytics for non-profits], and I believe the knowledge and critical thinking skills I can gain from your class will be instrumental in achieving that.

I am eager to engage deeply with the material, participate actively in discussions, and learn from your expertise. I am particularly interested in [mention a specific topic within the course you hope to explore further].

Thank you for your time and for teaching this course. I look forward to a productive semester.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]
[Your Student ID Number]<turn|>
